segments.text: Then Job responded:
If my grief could be weighed and my troubles placed on the scales
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: they would be heavier than the sand of the sea. That's why I spoke so rashly.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For the arrows of the Almighty are in me; their poison saps my spirit. God's terrors are lined[^26] up against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Don't wild donkeys bray when their grass is gone? Don't cattle groan when they don't have food! [^27]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Can something that's tasteless be eaten without salt? Is there any taste in the white of an egg?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I just can't touch any food—even the thought makes me feel sick!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Oh, if only I could have what I really want, that God would give me what I most desire—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that God would be willing to crush me to death, that he would just let me die!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But it still comforts me to know, making me happy through the never-ending pain, that I have never rejected the words of God. [^28]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why should I go on waiting when I don't have the strength? Why should I keep going when I don't know what is going to happen to me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Am I as strong as rock? Am I made out of bronze?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How can I help myself now that any chance of success is ripped[^29] away from me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Anyone who isn't kind to a friend has given up respecting the Almighty.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My brothers[^30] have acted as deceptively as a desert stream, rushing waters in the desert that vanish.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The stream floods when it is full of dark ice and melting snow,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: but in the heat it dries up and disappears, vanishing from where it once was.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Camel caravans turn aside to look for water, but don't find any and they die.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Caravans from Tema looked, travelers from Sheba were confident,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: but their hopes were dashed—they came and found nothing.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Now you are no help, just like that[^31]—you see my trouble and you're afraid.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Have I asked you for anything? Have I told you to bribe[^32] anyone for me from your wealth?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Have I asked you to rescue me from an enemy? Have I told you to save me from my oppressors?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Explain this to me, and I'll be quiet. Show me where I'm wrong.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Honest words are painful, [^33] but what do your arguments prove?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Are you going to argue over what I said, when the words of someone in despair should be left to blow away in the wind?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You would play dice to win an orphan; you would bargain away your friend!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Look me in the eye and see if I'm lying to your face!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Don't talk like this! [^34] Don't be unjust! What I'm saying is right.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I'm not telling lies—don't you think I wouldn't know if I was wrong?
segments.speaker: Job
